========================================================================
SUPRAVISOR — FULL DOCUMENTATION
Powered by the open Uniplex protocol
Last updated: 2026-02-24
API: https://uniplex.ai/api
Docs: https://docs.uniplex.ai
========================================================================

OVERVIEW
--------
Supravisor is the trust layer for AI agents. It answers three fundamental questions about any AI agent operating in your systems:

1. Who is this agent? (Identity via Passports)
2. What is it allowed to do? (Policy via Gates and Guardrails)
3. What did it actually do? (Audit via Attestations)

Every AI agent — Claude, GPT-4, a custom Python script, an AutoGen workflow — can be issued a cryptographic passport, evaluated against a set of constraints before each action, and have every decision recorded in a tamper-proof audit trail.

Supravisor is built on the open Uniplex protocol. The cryptography (Ed25519 signatures) is standard and independently verifiable. You are never locked into trusting Supravisor's word — every attestation can be verified offline using the gate's public key.

========================================================================
SECTION 1: CORE CONCEPTS
========================================================================

ISSUERS
-------
An issuer is the signing authority in Supravisor's trust chain. Think of it as a certificate authority, but purpose-built for AI agent credentials rather than TLS.

Every passport must be signed by an issuer. Gates verify passports by checking whether the signing issuer is in their trusted list. This means trust is explicit: you configure which issuers you trust, and only credentials from those issuers are accepted.

Issuer ID format: issuer:yourdomain.ai (or issuer:personal.local for dev)

Trust tiers:
- Self (L1): Anyone can create a self-issued passport. For development only.
- Internal (L2): Issued through your Supravisor account. For production.
- Verified (coming): Domain-verified via DNS/HTTPS challenge. Cross-org trust.
- Certified (coming): KYB-verified for regulated industries.

The Ed25519 keypair is generated in the browser (client-side). The private key is shown once and never stored by Supravisor. Only the holder of the private key can sign valid passports — even if Supravisor's servers were compromised, forging passports would be cryptographically impossible.

One issuer can sign many passports. Revoking an issuer immediately invalidates all passports it signed.

PASSPORTS
---------
A passport is a signed JSON credential that an AI agent carries to prove its identity and permissions. It has two components: a payload (the claims) and a signature (the proof).

Example passport structure:
{
  "payload": {
    "passport_id": "uni_abc123",
    "agent_id": "my-research-agent",
    "agent_name": "Research Bot",
    "issuer_id": "issuer:mycompany.ai",
    "permissions": ["web:search", "mcp:read"],
    "public_key": "base64_ed25519_public_key...",
    "issued_at": "2026-02-24T00:00:00Z",
    "expires_at": "2027-02-24T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "signature": "Ed25519-base64_signature_over_payload..."
}

Permission namespaces:
- mcp:* — MCP tool calls (mcp:read, mcp:write, mcp:search)
- web:* — Web browsing (web:search, web:read, web:fetch)
- email:* — Email operations (email:read, email:send, email:delete)
- files:* — File system (files:read, files:write, files:delete)
- payments:* — Payment operations (payments:read, payments:send)
- api:* — Generic API calls (api:read, api:write, api:admin)
- tools:* — Tool use (tools:search, tools:execute)
- * — Full access (use with extreme caution)

Private keys:
The agent's private key is generated in the browser when a passport is issued. It's shown exactly once. The standard environment variable is UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY. This key is used for proof-of-possession at L3.

Self-issued L1 passports (no account needed):
  from uniplex import Agent
  agent = Agent.create("my-agent", permissions=["mcp:*"])

Loading from environment:
  agent = Agent.from_env()  # reads UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY

Passports expire at their expires_at timestamp. Recommended TTLs: 365d (dev), 90d (production), 30d (high-security). Revocation is instant — revoked passports are rejected at every gate check regardless of expiry.

GATES
-----
A gate is the local enforcement checkpoint between an agent and a resource. Every action request goes through a gate:
1. Parse the passport token
2. Verify the Ed25519 signature
3. Check the issuer is trusted
4. Check the passport isn't expired
5. Check the passport isn't revoked (L2: cached; L3: live)
6. Verify permissions include the requested action
7. Evaluate all configured guardrail constraints
8. Sign and store an attestation record
9. Return allow/deny with reason

Step 7 (constraint evaluation) happens locally with no network call. This is why gates add under 1ms to the request path regardless of network conditions.

Gate configuration:
- trusted_issuers: list of issuer IDs to accept
- profile: L1, L2, or L3
- constraints: the guardrail configuration (see below)
- required_permissions: passport must include these

Decision outcomes always include:
- allowed: boolean
- reason: human-readable explanation (always present)
- attestation_id: ID of the signed record

GUARDRAILS
----------
Guardrails are the 17 constraint evaluators that govern how agents operate beyond simple permissions.

Permissions are binary: can/can't. Guardrails are continuous: up to $50/day, max 10/minute, only these domains, never delete files.

All 17 constraint types:

1. max_per_action — Spending cap per single call — "$5.00"
2. max_per_minute — Rate limit actions per minute — "10"
3. max_per_hour — Rate limit actions per hour — "100"
4. max_per_day — Daily spending cap — "$50.00"
5. max_cumulative — Lifetime cumulative spend cap — "$500.00"
6. cumulative_window — Rolling window for cumulative tracking — "30d"
7. approval_threshold — Human approval required above this — "$25.00"
8. domain_allowlist — Only allow these domains — "openai.com,anthropic.com"
9. domain_blocklist — Always block these domains — "facebook.com,twitter.com"
10. action_allowlist — Only these specific actions permitted — "web:search,mcp:read"
11. action_blocklist — These actions always denied — "files:delete,payments:send"
12. read_only — Block all write/mutate operations — true/false
13. no_pii_export — Detect and block PII in payloads — true/false
14. required — Request must include this metadata field — "user_id"
15. model — Require a specific AI model — "claude-3-5-sonnet"
16. response_time_ms — SLA latency requirement — "2000"
17. uptime_basis_points — SLA uptime — "9900" (99%)

Evaluation order: signature → expiry → revocation → issuer trust → permission → action_blocklist → action_allowlist → domain_blocklist → domain_allowlist → read_only → required → model → no_pii_export → max_per_action → rate limits → spend limits → approval_threshold

All constraints are AND-evaluated. The first failure stops evaluation and returns a denial with the specific reason.

TRUST PROFILES
--------------
L1 — Baseline:
- Accepts self-issued passports (no account needed)
- Verifies signature and expiry
- Does NOT check issuer trust
- Does NOT protect against replay attacks
- Does NOT check revocation
- Use for: local development, prototyping, quick experiments
- Warning: Never use L1 in production

L2 — Standard:
- Requires issuer in trusted list
- Nonce-based replay attack protection
- Cached revocation check (60s TTL)
- No network call for authorization (cached revocation only)
- Use for: all production agents, external-facing services, team deployments

L3 — Strict:
- Everything L2 checks, plus:
- Proof-of-Possession: agent must sign {passport_id}:{nonce}:{timestamp} with its private key
- Live revocation check on every request (adds ~10-50ms)
- Timestamp must be within 30 seconds
- Use for: financial agents, healthcare, payroll, anything high-stakes

ATTESTATIONS
------------
An attestation is a signed, immutable record of every gate decision. It is signed by the gate's Ed25519 private key at the moment of decision. The signature covers the entire document — any field modification breaks the signature.

Full attestation structure:
{
  "attestation_id": "att_xyz789",
  "decision": "allow",
  "reason": "All constraints satisfied",
  "passport_id": "uni_abc123",
  "agent_id": "my-research-agent",
  "gate_id": "gate_def456",
  "action": "mcp:search",
  "resource": "web:google",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-24T10:30:00Z",
  "trust_profile": "L2",
  "constraints_evaluated": [
    {"type": "max_per_day", "value": "$50", "current": "$12.40", "result": "pass"}
  ],
  "gate_signature": "Ed25519-base64..."
}

Attestations cannot be altered. They can be shared via public URLs that show the decision without exposing sensitive configuration. They can be exported as JSON or CSV for compliance audits. The gate's public key (available from the dashboard) can be used for offline signature verification.

========================================================================
SECTION 2: SDKS
========================================================================

PYTHON SDK
----------
Install: pip install uniplex
Requires: Python 3.8+

Quick start:
  from uniplex import Agent, Gate, TrustProfile
  agent = Agent.from_env()
  gate = Gate(profile=TrustProfile.L2, trusted_issuers=["issuer:mycompany.ai"])
  token = agent.generate_token()
  decision = gate.authorize(token, "web:search")
  if decision.allowed:
      print(f"Authorized! Attestation: {decision.attestation_id}")

Agent class:
  Agent.create(agent_id, permissions, issuer_id=None) → Agent (self-issued L1)
  Agent(passport_id, private_key) → Agent (load existing)
  Agent.from_env() → Agent (reads UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY)
  Agent.from_json(json_str) → Agent
  agent.authorize(action, resource=None, metadata=None) → Decision
  agent.generate_token(nonce=None, metadata=None) → str
  agent.passport_id → str
  agent.agent_id → str
  agent.permissions → list[str]
  agent.expires_at → datetime
  agent.issuer_id → str

Gate class:
  Gate(profile, trusted_issuers=None, constraints=None, gate_id=None)
  gate.authorize(passport_token, action, resource=None, metadata=None) → Decision
  gate.verify(passport_token) → VerificationResult

Decision class:
  decision.allowed → bool
  decision.reason → str
  decision.attestation_id → str | None
  decision.constraints_evaluated → list[dict]

Environment variables:
  UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY — Agent's Ed25519 private key (base64)
  UNIPLEX_API_KEY — API key (uni_xxxx) for managed platform
  UNIPLEX_GATE_ID — Default gate for agent.authorize()

Error types:
  PassportExpiredError — passport past expires_at
  PassportRevokedError — passport was manually revoked
  InsufficientPermissionsError — passport lacks required permission
  RateLimitExceededError — max_per_minute or max_per_hour exceeded (.retry_after_seconds)
  SpendingLimitExceededError — max_per_day, max_per_action, or max_cumulative exceeded
  UniplexError — base class for all Supravisor errors

TYPESCRIPT / NODE.JS SDK
------------------------
Install: npm install uniplex
Requires: Node.js 18+, TypeScript 4.9+

Quick start:
  import { Agent, Gate, TrustProfile } from 'uniplex';
  const agent = await Agent.fromEnv();
  const gate = new Gate({ profile: TrustProfile.L2, trustedIssuers: ['issuer:mycompany.ai'] });
  const token = agent.generateToken();
  const decision = await gate.authorize(token, 'web:search');

Key interfaces:
  interface Decision {
    allowed: boolean;
    reason: string;
    attestationId: string | null;
    constraintsEvaluated: ConstraintResult[];
    checkedAt: Date;
  }

  interface ConstraintResult {
    type: string;
    value: string;
    current: string;
    result: 'pass' | 'fail';
    detail: string;
  }

Agent class (TypeScript):
  Agent.create(agentId, { permissions, expiresIn?, agentName?, issuerId? }) → Promise<Agent>
  new Agent({ passportId, privateKey }) → Agent
  Agent.fromEnv() → Promise<Agent>
  Agent.fromJSON(json) → Agent
  agent.authorize(action, options?) → Promise<Decision>
  agent.generateToken(options?) → string
  agent.passportId → string
  agent.permissions → string[]
  agent.expiresAt → Date

Gate class (TypeScript):
  new Gate({ profile, trustedIssuers?, constraints?, gateId?, apiKey? })
  gate.authorize(passportToken, action, options?) → Promise<Decision>
  gate.verify(passportToken) → Promise<VerificationResult>

Constraints interface (TypeScript):
  maxPerAction?: string         // "$5.00"
  maxPerMinute?: number         // 10
  maxPerHour?: number           // 100
  maxPerDay?: string            // "$50.00"
  maxCumulative?: string        // "$500.00"
  cumulativeWindow?: string     // "30d"
  approvalThreshold?: string    // "$25.00"
  domainAllowlist?: string      // "openai.com,anthropic.com"
  domainBlocklist?: string      // "facebook.com,twitter.com"
  actionAllowlist?: string[]    // ["web:search", "mcp:read"]
  actionBlocklist?: string[]    // ["files:delete", "payments:send"]
  readOnly?: boolean            // true
  noPiiExport?: boolean         // true
  required?: string             // "user_id"
  model?: string                // "claude-3-5-sonnet"
  responseTimeMs?: number       // 2000
  uptimeBasisPoints?: number    // 9900

========================================================================
SECTION 3: MCP SERVER
========================================================================

The Supravisor MCP server exposes 21 tools via Streamable HTTP transport with OAuth 2.0 PKCE authentication. Any MCP-compatible AI client (Claude, Claude Code) can manage the entire Supravisor platform through natural language.

Installation: npx uniplex-mcp-manage

First run: opens browser for OAuth 2.0 PKCE authorization (one time). Token stored at ~/.uniplex/mcp-token.json. Tokens valid 30 days, auto-refresh.

Claude Desktop config:
  {
    "mcpServers": {
      "supravisor": {
        "command": "npx",
        "args": ["uniplex-mcp-manage"],
        "env": { "UNIPLEX_API_KEY": "uni_xxxx" }
      }
    }
  }

All 21 tools:

Issuer Management (5):
  create_issuer — Create a new signing authority
  list_issuers — List all issuers with trust tier and status
  get_issuer — Get full details of a specific issuer
  update_issuer — Update issuer name or description
  revoke_issuer — Revoke an issuer and all its passports

Passport Management (4):
  issue_passport — Issue a new passport for an agent
  list_passports — List passports with optional filters
  get_passport — Get passport details
  revoke_passport — Revoke a passport immediately

Gate Management (5):
  create_gate — Create a new enforcement gate
  list_gates — List all gates with configurations
  get_gate — Get full gate config including constraints
  update_gate — Update gate configuration
  delete_gate — Delete a gate

Authorization (2):
  check_authorization — Check if a passport is authorized for an action
  verify_passport — Verify passport signature and validity

Attestations (3):
  list_attestations — List attestations with filters
  get_attestation — Get single attestation with full detail
  export_attestations — Export as JSON or CSV

Templates (2):
  list_templates — List all available constraint templates
  apply_template — Apply a template to a gate

Example Claude prompts:
  "Issue a passport for my research agent with web:search permissions, expiring in 90 days"
  "What gates do I have and which issuers do they trust?"
  "Show me the last 10 authorization decisions for my agent"
  "Apply the Read-Only Researcher template to my research gate"
  "Rotate the passport for agent research-bot before it expires next week"
  "Export all denials from last month as CSV"

========================================================================
SECTION 4: API REFERENCE
========================================================================

BASE URL: https://uniplex.ai/api

AUTHENTICATION:
  Header: Authorization: Bearer uni_xxxx
  Format: uni_ prefix + 64 lowercase hex chars = 68 chars total

RATE LIMITS:
  Authorization checks: 100/min
  Passport creation: 30/min
  Gate creation: 10/min
  All other: 60/min
  Headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset

ERROR FORMAT: {"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_CODE"}

SCOPES:
  gates:create, gates:read, gates:update, gates:delete, gates:manage
  passports:issue, passports:read, passports:revoke
  issuers:create, issuers:read, issuers:update
  guardrails:create, guardrails:read, guardrails:update
  templates:create, templates:read, templates:update
  audit:read, audit:export, audit:share
  osgate:read, osgate:write, osgate:manage
  admin:*

ENDPOINTS:

POST /api/authorize — Check authorization (CORE ENDPOINT)
  Request: { passport_token, action, resource?, gate_id?, metadata? }
  Response: { allowed, reason, attestation_id, checked_at, constraints_evaluated }
  Scope: any valid API key

POST /api/passports — Issue passport
  Request: { agent_id, agent_name, issuer_id, permissions, expires_in? }
  Response: { passport_id, private_key (ONCE), passport_token, ... }
  Scope: passports:issue

GET /api/passports — List passports
  Params: agent_id, issuer_id, status, expiring_before, limit, offset
  Scope: passports:read

GET /api/passports/:id — Get passport
  Scope: passports:read

DELETE /api/passports/:id — Revoke passport
  Response: { passport_id, status: "revoked", revoked_at }
  Scope: passports:revoke

POST /api/issuers — Create issuer
  Request: { name, issuer_id, description?, public_key? }
  Response: { issuer_id, private_key (ONCE), public_key, ... }
  Scope: issuers:create

GET /api/issuers — List issuers
  Scope: issuers:read

GET /api/issuers/:id — Get issuer
  Scope: issuers:read

PUT /api/issuers/:id — Update issuer
  Scope: issuers:update

DELETE /api/issuers/:id — Revoke issuer (invalidates ALL passports)
  Scope: issuers:update

POST /api/gates — Create gate
  Request: { name, profile, trusted_issuers?, constraints?, template? }
  Response: { gate_id, public_key (for attestation verification), ... }
  Scope: gates:create

GET /api/gates — List gates
  Scope: gates:read

GET /api/gates/:id — Get gate
  Scope: gates:read

PUT /api/gates/:id — Update gate
  Scope: gates:update

DELETE /api/gates/:id — Delete gate
  Scope: gates:delete

GET /api/gates/:id/public-key — Get gate's public key for offline attestation verification
  Scope: gates:read

POST /api/guardrails — Create guardrail config
  Scope: guardrails:create

GET /api/guardrails — List guardrail configs
  Scope: guardrails:read

GET /api/guardrails/:id — Get guardrail config
  Scope: guardrails:read

PUT /api/guardrails/:id — Update guardrail config
  Scope: guardrails:update

POST /api/guardrails/:id/apply — Apply to gates
  Scope: guardrails:update, gates:update

POST /api/guardrails/validate — Validate a constraint config (no scope required)

GET /api/audit-trail — List attestations
  Params: agent_id, gate_id, passport_id, decision, action, from, to, limit, offset, sort
  Scope: audit:read

GET /api/audit-trail/:id — Get single attestation (full detail)
  Scope: audit:read

POST /api/audit-trail/:id/share — Create public share link
  Request: { expires_in? }
  Response: { share_url, expires_at }
  Scope: audit:share

GET /api/audit-trail/export — Export as JSON or CSV
  Params: format (json|csv), include_constraints, + filter params
  Scope: audit:export

GET /api/audit-trail/stats — Aggregate statistics
  Scope: audit:read

COMMON ERROR CODES:
  UNAUTHORIZED (401) — Missing or invalid API key
  FORBIDDEN (403) — API key lacks required scope
  NOT_FOUND (404) — Resource does not exist
  VALIDATION_ERROR (422) — Request body failed validation
  RATE_LIMITED (429) — Rate limit exceeded (check Retry-After header)
  PASSPORT_EXPIRED (400) — Passport past expires_at
  PASSPORT_REVOKED (400) — Passport was revoked
  ISSUER_NOT_TRUSTED (403) — Passport issuer not in gate's trusted list
  PERMISSION_DENIED (403) — Passport lacks required permission
  CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION (403) — Guardrail constraint blocked the action
  SIGNATURE_INVALID (400) — Passport or token signature is invalid
  INTERNAL_ERROR (500) — Unexpected server error

========================================================================
SECTION 5: INTEGRATION GUIDES
========================================================================

PROTECTING CLAUDE AND CLAUDE CODE
----------------------------------
Claude is powerful — and that's exactly why it needs guardrails. A Claude session with file system access can read, write, and delete files. Claude Code can run arbitrary shell commands. Without Supravisor, there's no audit trail and no enforcement.

Option A: Python SDK (for custom Claude tooling):
  Wrap tool calls with gate.authorize() before execution. If the decision is not allowed, return the error to Claude rather than executing the tool.

Option B: MCP Server (recommended):
  Run: npx uniplex-mcp-manage
  Add to Claude settings → MCP Servers → add Supravisor
  Set UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY and UNIPLEX_GATE_ID in Claude's environment

Setting environment variables for Claude Code:
  export UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY="ed25519_private_key_here"
  export UNIPLEX_GATE_ID="gate_def456"
  
  Or in project .env:
  UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY=ed25519_private_key_here
  UNIPLEX_GATE_ID=gate_def456

Troubleshooting Claude denials:
  "Domain not in allowlist" → add domain to gate's domain_allowlist
  "Spending limit exceeded" → increase max_per_day or wait until tomorrow
  "Rate limit exceeded" → increase max_per_minute
  "Passport expired" → issue new passport, update UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY
  "Issuer not trusted" → add issuer to gate's trusted_issuers

PROTECTING OPENAI AGENTS
-------------------------
The core pattern for OpenAI Agents SDK — check authorization before executing any tool:

  from uniplex import Agent
  agent = Agent.from_env()
  
  def safe_tool_call(tool_name: str, fn, **kwargs):
      decision = agent.authorize(f"tools:{tool_name}")
      if not decision.allowed:
          raise PermissionError(f"Blocked: {decision.reason}")
      return fn(**kwargs)

For the Assistants API: in the required_action loop, call gate.authorize() before execute tool, and submit the denial as the tool output if not allowed.

Common patterns:
  Spend-limited GPT: { max_per_action: "$2.00", max_per_day: "$25.00", approval_threshold: "$10.00" }
  Read-only researcher: { read_only: true, domain_allowlist: "arxiv.org,..," }
  Rate-limited support: { max_per_minute: 5, action_allowlist: ["email:read", "email:send", "tools:search_kb"] }

AGENT AUTONOMY
--------------
The bootstrap problem: a human must set up the trust infrastructure exactly once. After that, agents operate autonomously.

Human does once:
  1. Create Supravisor account
  2. Generate API key → store in secrets manager
  3. Create issuer → store issuer private key
  4. Configure gates

Agent does ongoing:
  - Issue sub-agent passports (using UNIPLEX_API_KEY)
  - Rotate credentials before expiry
  - Verify other agents' passports
  - Read own audit trail
  - Configure gates (if given gates:update scope)

Path A — Self-issued L1 (no account):
  from uniplex import Agent
  agent = Agent.create("my-agent", permissions=["mcp:*"])
  decision = agent.authorize("mcp:search")

Path B — Managed platform (recommended for production):
  from uniplex import UniplexClient
  client = UniplexClient(api_key=os.environ["UNIPLEX_API_KEY"])
  passport = client.passports.issue(agent_id="worker-001", permissions=["web:search"], expires_in="90d")

Path C — MCP server with OAuth (for Claude):
  npx uniplex-mcp-manage  # agent instructs Claude to manage credentials

Agent-to-agent trust: use gate.verify(token) to verify another agent's passport before accepting work from it.

Autonomous credential rotation:
  Check (agent.expires_at - now).days < 7, then call client.passports.issue() for a new passport, update secrets manager, call client.passports.revoke(old_id).

AUDIT TRAIL AND SESSION SHARING
---------------------------------
Every gate decision is in the audit trail as a signed attestation.

Dashboard: Activity tab (real-time stream) vs Audit Trail tab (full queryable history).

API query:
  GET /api/audit-trail?agent_id=my-agent&decision=deny&limit=50
  Headers: Authorization: Bearer uni_xxxx

Sharing: POST /api/audit-trail/:id/share → returns share_url
  Public URL shows: decision, action, timestamp, constraints (pass/fail), signature
  Public URL hides: API keys, gate internals, issuer private keys

Export: GET /api/audit-trail/export?format=csv&from=2026-01-01

Offline verification (Python):
  signature = base64.b64decode(attestation.pop("gate_signature"))
  payload = json.dumps(attestation, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')).encode()
  Ed25519PublicKey.from_public_bytes(base64.b64decode(gate_pub_key)).verify(signature, payload)

Use cases: compliance audits (export signed JSON for auditors), client reporting (share URLs), debugging (find denial + read constraints_evaluated), dispute resolution, billing verification.

========================================================================
SECTION 6: CONSTRAINT TEMPLATES
========================================================================

30 pre-built constraint configurations across 5 categories. Apply via dashboard → Gates → Create Gate → Choose Template. Or via API with "template": "template-name" in the gate creation request.

PERSONAL PRODUCTIVITY (6):
  personal-email-agent — 50/day, no delete, no bulk PII export
  calendar-manager — 20/day create/update, no delete
  file-organizer — read/move/rename only, no delete, no external sharing
  note-taking-agent — create/edit notes, sensitive folders read-only
  task-manager — create/update/complete, no delete
  personal-shopper — $500/action cap, $100 approval threshold

BUSINESS OPERATIONS (6):
  customer-support-agent — KB + ticket read/respond, escalate on keywords
  sales-development-agent — research + draft, no autonomous email send
  contract-review-agent — read-only PDFs/docs, no external AI upload
  hr-onboarding-agent — template emails only, no SSN/salary fields
  expense-report-agent — read receipts + create records, no payment execution
  meeting-scheduler — calendar R/W, limited external invites

DEVELOPER TOOLS (6):
  code-review-agent — git read + PR comments, no push/merge
  cicd-agent — trigger test pipelines, read logs, no config changes
  documentation-generator — read code + write docs, no code changes
  bug-triage-agent — read + label + assign, no close/delete
  security-scanner — read-only codebase, flag CVEs, no auto-fix
  dependency-auditor — npm/pip audit read-only, report only

FINANCE & COMMERCE (6):
  budget-tracker — read-only transactions + reports
  invoice-processor — read + record, $1000 approval threshold, no payments
  financial-research-agent — read-only market data, no trades
  crypto-trading-agent — L3, $100/trade, $500/day, $50 approval, full audit
  payroll-assistant — L3, no SSN export, all actions require approval
  subscription-manager — cancel with approval, no new subscriptions

RESEARCH & CONTENT (6):
  web-research-agent — search + read, configurable domain allowlist
  content-writer — draft only, no publish
  social-media-manager — 10 posts/day, no delete
  data-analyst — read + aggregate, no raw PII export
  market-research-agent — public web only, no internal data
  competitive-intelligence-agent — public web only, no logins

========================================================================
SECTION 7: QUICK REFERENCE
========================================================================

ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES:
  UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY — Agent's private key (base64 Ed25519)
  UNIPLEX_API_KEY — API key (uni_xxxx) for managed operations
  UNIPLEX_GATE_ID — Default gate ID for agent.authorize()
  UNIPLEX_ISSUER_PRIVATE_KEY — Issuer's signing key for passport issuance

TRUST PROFILE DECISION GUIDE:
  Development / prototyping → L1
  Financial / healthcare / high-stakes → L3
  Everything else production → L2

PERMISSION GRANT BEST PRACTICES:
  - Never grant * in production
  - Use specific namespaces (web:search not web:*)
  - Add action_blocklist as defense-in-depth
  - Review permissions when rotating credentials

GATE CONSTRAINTS QUICK PATTERNS:
  Read-only research: { read_only: true, domain_allowlist: "...", no_pii_export: true }
  Spend-limited: { max_per_action: "$5", max_per_day: "$50", approval_threshold: "$25" }
  Rate-limited: { max_per_minute: 10, max_per_hour: 100 }
  Social media blocked: { domain_blocklist: "facebook.com,twitter.com,instagram.com" }
  Files no delete: { action_blocklist: ["files:delete", "files:move"] }

ATTESTATION FACT SHEET:
  - Signed by gate's Ed25519 private key at decision time
  - Cannot be altered after creation (signature breaks)
  - Always includes decision, reason, constraints_evaluated
  - Public share URL available (shows result, hides internals)
  - Exportable as JSON or CSV
  - Independently verifiable with gate's public key

CONTACT / LINKS:
  Docs: https://docs.uniplex.ai
  API: https://uniplex.ai/api
  Dashboard: https://uniplex.ai
  GitHub: https://github.com/StandardLogic/supravisor-docs
  Protocol: https://uniplex.ai/protocol

========================================================================
END OF SUPRAVISOR FULL DOCUMENTATION
========================================================================
